events.title: Fuel oil leak at Caribbean Petroleum reaches Las Lajas Creek and Ciénaga Las Cucharillas (Cataño, Puerto Rico)
events.description: EPA reported that a storage tank at the Caribbean Petroleum facility leaked more than 16,000 gallons of fuel oil, with more than 800 gallons reaching Las Lajas Creek, a tributary of the Ciénaga Las Cucharillas marsh. EPA later described enforcement concerns including delayed notification and apparent equipment/containment issues.

sources.headline: EPA proposes $291,000 in penalties against 4 companies for failing to report hazardous substance releases in Puerto Rico during Hurricane Georges

key_quotes.quote: leaked more than 16,000 gallons of fuel oil; more than 800 gallons reached Las Lajas Creek

events.title: Caustic soda spill from storage tank at El Morro Corrugated Box (Vega Alta, Puerto Rico)
events.description: EPA reported that approximately 1,700 gallons of caustic soda spilled from a storage tank at El Morro Corrugated Box on or about September 21, 1998. The incident was cited by EPA in an enforcement context related to reporting and response notifications after Hurricane Georges.

sources.headline: EPA proposes $291,000 in penalties against 4 companies for failing to report hazardous substance releases in Puerto Rico during Hurricane Georges

key_quotes.quote: approximately 1,700 gallons of caustic soda spilled from a storage tank on or about September 21, 1998

events.title: Four 2,000‑lb chlorine cylinders reported released/lost at PRASA facility (Jayuya, Puerto Rico)
events.description: EPA reported that four 2,000‑pound chlorine cylinders were released or lost from a PRASA facility around September 21, 1998. EPA stated that not all were recovered and that delayed notification could have posed risk if cylinders ruptured.

sources.headline: EPA proposes $291,000 in penalties against 4 companies for failing to report hazardous substance releases in Puerto Rico during Hurricane Georges

key_quotes.quote: Four 2,000-pound chlorine cylinders were released/lost... not all were recovered

events.title: Hazardous-substance drums swept away by floodwaters at Lilly del Caribe (Mayagüez, Puerto Rico)
events.description: EPA reported that nearly 50 drums, almost half reported to contain hazardous substances, were swept away in floodwaters during September 21–22, 1998 at Lilly del Caribe in Mayagüez. EPA later cited delayed reporting and inventory shortcomings as part of enforcement actions.

sources.headline: EPA proposes $291,000 in penalties against 4 companies for failing to report hazardous substance releases in Puerto Rico during Hurricane Georges

key_quotes.quote: Nearly 50 drums... were swept away in floodwaters on September 21-22, 1998

events.title: Tropical Storm Frances landfall near Corpus Christi, Texas caused heavy rain and flooding
events.description: Tropical Storm Frances developed in the Gulf of Mexico and made landfall just north of Corpus Christi, Texas on September 11, 1998. The storm produced widespread heavy rainfall and flooding impacts across parts of the U.S. Gulf Coast during Sept 8–13.

sources.headline: 1998 Tropical Storm Frances

key_quotes.quote: made landfall just north of Corpus Christi, Texas on September 11, 1998

events.title: Hurricane Georges struck Puerto Rico causing flooding and landslides
events.description: Hurricane Georges struck Puerto Rico on the evening of September 21, 1998, bringing heavy rainfall. USGS documentation describes major flooding in Arecibo and Utuado on September 22, 1998.

sources.headline: Flood of September 22, 1998, Arecibo and Utuado, Puerto Rico
USGS Water-Resources Investigations Report (WRI 01-4247)

key_quotes.quote: Flood of September 22, 1998, Arecibo and Utuado, Puerto Rico

events.title: Hurricane Georges made U.S. Gulf Coast landfall near Biloxi, Mississippi
events.description: Hurricane Georges made landfall near Biloxi, Mississippi in late September 1998 and then moved inland slowly. The storm produced damaging winds and freshwater flooding in parts of Mississippi and Alabama.

sources.headline: Hurricane Georges
1998 Hurricane Georges Advisory Archive

dedupe.cluster_hint: Hurricane Georges U.S. Gulf Coast landfall

events.title: Typhoon Vicki recurved and made landfall on Japan’s Kii Peninsula after crossing Luzon
events.description: Typhoon Vicki formed in mid-September 1998, crossed Luzon in the Philippines, and then recurved toward Japan. It made landfall on the Kii Peninsula on September 22 before transitioning to an extratropical system around September 23.

sources.headline: Typhoon Vicki (199807) summary
Journal article (JMSJ) related to Typhoon Vicki

key_quotes.quote: made landfall on the Kii Peninsula on September 22

events.title: Monsoon flooding persisted across Bangladesh through September 1998
events.description: Severe monsoon flooding that began earlier in 1998 continued into September, affecting large portions of Bangladesh. An FAO update dated September 1998 described extensive flooding with major impacts on people and agriculture.

sources.headline: Bangladesh situation update (September 1998)

key_quotes.quote: Severe monsoon flooding that began in July continued through September 1998

events.title: Pymatuning earthquake (mbLg 5.2) near Jamestown, Pennsylvania
events.description: A mbLg 5.2 earthquake occurred near Jamestown, Pennsylvania on September 25, 1998. USGS reports noted minor damage and documented hydrologic effects, including changes to wells and springs.

sources.headline: Preliminary results of the investigation of the Pymatuning earthquake of September 25, 1998
Hydrologic effects of the Pymatuning earthquake

key_quotes.quote: Preliminary results of the investigation of the Pymatuning earthquake of September 25, 1998
Hydrologic effects of the Pymatuning earthquake

events.title: Earthquake reported in central Serbia (UPI report)
events.description: A UPI report described an earthquake in central Serbia on September 29, 1998, including reports of damage and casualties. The report described the event location generally as central Serbia without precise coordinates in the draft.

sources.headline: Earthquake shakes Serbia

dedupe.fingerprint: geological|earthquake reported in central serbia (upi report)|rs|1998-09-29

events.title: Juniper Flats wildfire in Riverside County, California destroys homes and structures
events.description: News reports said lightning sparked multiple wildfires across Southern California during a heat wave. The most destructive blaze in Riverside County near Juniper Flats destroyed homes and other structures amid extreme heat and erratic winds.

location.name: Juniper Flats area, Riverside County, California, USA

sources.headline: Lightning Sparks Fires in Southern California
la-xpm-1998-sep-02-mn-18830-story

events.title: Santiago Canyon fire in Orange County, California spreads rapidly after lightning ignition
events.description: Los Angeles Times reports said a lightning-sparked brush fire in or near Santiago Canyon grew to thousands of acres during a heat wave. Later reports indicated officials expected full containment as rainfall aided firefighting efforts.

location.name: Santiago Canyon / Foothill Ranch area, Orange County, California, USA

sources.headline: la-xpm-1998-sep-03-mn-19104-story
la-xpm-1998-sep-05-me-19689-story

events.title: Wildfire on Marine Corps Base Camp Pendleton burns about 2,000 acres and threatens nearby napalm storage area
events.description: The Washington Post reported that lightning ignited multiple fires across Southern California. One blaze on Marine Corps Base Camp Pendleton burned about 2,000 acres and advanced toward, but did not reach, a napalm storage installation.

location.name: Marine Corps Base Camp Pendleton, California, USA

sources.headline: Lightning Sparks Fires in Southern California

events.title: Wildfire near Barona Indian Reservation in San Diego County burns about 4,800 acres; casino closes temporarily
events.description: The Washington Post reported that lightning sparked a cluster of Southern California brush fires during a heat wave. One fire on or near the Barona Indian Reservation in San Diego County burned roughly 4,800 acres and prompted a temporary precautionary closure of the tribe’s casino.

location.name: Barona Indian Reservation area, San Diego County, California, USA

sources.headline: Lightning Sparks Fires in Southern California

events.title: NMFS designates critical habitat for green and hawksbill sea turtles in Puerto Rico waters
events.description: NOAA/NMFS issued a final rule under the U.S. Endangered Species Act designating marine critical habitat for threatened green sea turtles and endangered hawksbill sea turtles in coastal waters around Culebra Island and around Mona and Monito Islands (Puerto Rico). The rule identifies habitat important to conservation, including areas associated with seagrass beds and coral reefs.

sources.headline: NMFS final rule designating critical habitat for green and hawksbill sea turtles in Puerto Rico waters (Culebra; Mona & Monito)

key_quotes.quote: issued a final rule ... designating ... critical habitat ... around Culebra Island; Mona and Monito Islands

dedupe.cluster_hint: 1998-09 ESA critical habitat sea turtles Puerto Rico

events.title: USFWS lists four Sierra Nevada foothills plant species as threatened under the ESA
events.description: The U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service published a final rule listing four plant species in California’s Sierra Nevada foothills as threatened under the Endangered Species Act. The rule cites threats including development, grazing/trampling, mining and recreation impacts, roadway maintenance, off-highway vehicle use, and small population vulnerability.

sources.headline: USFWS final rule listing four Sierra Nevada foothills plant species as threatened (ESA)

key_quotes.quote: final rule determining threatened status under the Endangered Species Act for four plant species

dedupe.cluster_hint: 1998-09 ESA threatened plants Sierra Nevada foothills California

events.title: EPA final rule revises Land Disposal Restrictions treatment standards for certain carbamate-production hazardous wastes
events.description: EPA published a final rule revising Land Disposal Restrictions (LDR) treatment standards for listed hazardous wastes from carbamate production, including alternative treatment standards for certain constituents and related CFR updates.

sources.headline: Emergency revision of Land Disposal Restrictions (LDR) treatment standards for listed hazardous wastes from carbamate production

key_quotes.quote: EPA is taking final action to revise the land disposal restrictions treatment standards for certain hazardous wastes... from carbamate production.

dedupe.cluster_hint: EPA LDR carbamate production final rule (Federal Register, 1998-09-04)

events.title: Executive Order 13101 directs federal agencies to increase waste prevention, recycling, and greener procurement
events.description: President Bill Clinton signed Executive Order 13101 directing executive agencies to incorporate waste prevention and recycling and to increase procurement of recycled and environmentally preferable products, with implementation roles defined for federal offices.

sources.headline: Greening the Government Through Waste Prevention, Recycling, and Federal Acquisition
Executive Order 13101—Greening the Government Through Waste Prevention, Recycling, and Federal Acquisition

key_quotes.quote: Greening the Government Through Waste Prevention, Recycling, and Federal Acquisition

events.title: EPA final rule requires regional NOx reductions to address interstate ozone transport in the Eastern U.S.
events.description: EPA announced a final plan requiring upwind states to reduce nitrogen oxide (NOx) emissions that contribute to downwind ozone pollution, affecting multiple Eastern states and the District of Columbia.

sources.source_name: U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) - Newsroom Archive
sources.headline: EPA Announces Final Rule For Regional NOx Reductions to Address Interstate Ozone Transport

key_quotes.quote: EPA today announced a final rule ... requiring significant reductions in nitrogen oxide (NOx) emissions ... to address interstate ozone transport.

dedupe.cluster_hint: EPA NOx SIP Call / interstate ozone transport final rule (1998-09-24)

events.title: EPA final rule sets Land Disposal Restrictions treatment standards for K088 spent potliners from primary aluminum production
events.description: EPA published a final rule under RCRA's Land Disposal Restrictions program establishing treatment standards for K088 spent potliners generated from primary aluminum reduction prior to land disposal.

sources.headline: Land Disposal Restrictions (LDR) treatment standards for spent potliners from primary aluminum reduction (K088)

key_quotes.quote: EPA is promulgating land disposal restrictions (LDR) treatment standards for spent potliners from primary aluminum reduction (K088).
